sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Li means to complete."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu glosses li as "establish" or "complete."

sentences.literal: 〉Yun fled into Chang'an and again plotted with Xian.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun slipped into Chang'an and took counsel again with Chen Xian.

sentences.chinese: 丞相具發其事，奏「咸宿衞執法之臣，幸得進見，漏泄所聞，以私語雲，爲定奏草，欲令自下治，〈師古曰：「咸爲御史中丞，而奏請下中丞，故云自下治。」
sentences.literal: The chancellor fully exposed the affair, memorializing: "Xian is a minister who guards the palace and upholds the law; he had the fortune to gain audience, leaked what he heard, spoke privately to Yun, drafted his memorial, and wished to have the matter sent down to himself for trial. 〈Yan's note: "Xian was Palace Secretary to the Imperial Counselor yet asked that the case go down to the assistant censor—hence 'send down to himself for trial.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: The chancellor laid out the whole intrigue: Chen Xian held a law officer's post, had heard state business in audience, leaked it to Zhu Yun, ghost-wrote his defense, and tried to route the case to his own bureau—Yan Shigu notes the irony of an assistant censor referring a case to himself.

sentences.chinese: 〉後知雲亡命罪人，而與交通，雲以故不得。」
sentences.literal: 〉Only afterward did he learn that Yun was a condemned fugitive, yet still trafficked with him—therefore Yun could not be taken."
sentences.idiomatic: He knew Zhu was a wanted fugitive yet conspired with him anyway—so Zhu could not be seized."

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "The clerks could not catch him."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: the officers could not run him to ground.

sentences.chinese: 〉上於是下咸、雲獄，減死爲城旦。
sentences.literal: 〉Thereupon the emperor cast Xian and Yun into prison, commuting execution to wall corvée.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor jailed both men and spared their lives for hard labor on the walls.

sentences.literal: Xian and Yun were then ruined and barred from office for the rest of Emperor Yuan's reign.
sentences.idiomatic: Both were disgraced and blocked from employment until Yuan died.

sentences.chinese: 至成帝時，丞相故安昌侯張禹以帝師位特進，甚尊重。
sentences.literal: By Emperor Cheng's time the former Marquis of Anchang, Zhang Yu, as the emperor's teacher held rank as specially advanced and was greatly honored.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Cheng, Zhang Yu of Anchang—once the emperor's tutor—sat as specially advanced minister and basked in deference.

sentences.literal: Yun submitted a memorial asking for audience; the dukes and ministers stood before him.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun demanded a hearing with the high ministers looking on.

sentences.chinese: 雲曰：「今朝庭大臣上不能匡主，下亡以益民，皆尸位素餐，〈師古曰：「尸，主也。
sentences.literal: Yun said: "The great ministers at court today neither correct the ruler above nor benefit the people below—they all hold a corpse's seat and eat empty salary. 〈Yan's note: "Shi means 'to preside.'"
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun said the high ministers neither steadied the throne nor helped the people—they were "corpse seat" placeholders drawing empty pay. Yan Shigu glosses shi as "preside."

sentences.literal: Su means empty.
sentences.idiomatic: Su means hollow or wasted.

sentences.chinese: 尸位者，不舉其事，但主其位而已。
sentences.literal: One who holds a corpse's seat does not advance the business but only occupies the place.
sentences.idiomatic: They warm the bench without doing the job.

sentences.chinese: 素餐者，德不稱官，空當食祿。」
sentences.literal: One who eats empty salary—his virtue does not match his office, yet he idly draws stipend."
sentences.idiomatic: They draw pay while their virtue never matches their rank."

sentences.chinese: 〉孔子所謂『鄙夫不可與事君』，『苟患失之，亡所不至』者也。
sentences.literal: 〉They are what Confucius called "the base fellow with whom one cannot serve a lord"—"once he fears losing favor, there is nothing he will not do."
sentences.idiomatic: They are the petty men Confucius warned against—clinging to place until they will commit any crime.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「皆論語所載孔子之言也。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "These are all sayings of Confucius recorded in the Analects.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: both quotations come from the Analects.

sentences.chinese: 苟患失其寵祿，則言行僻邪，無所不至也。」
sentences.literal: If he fears losing his favor and emolument, then his words and deeds turn crooked and there is nothing he will not reach to."
sentences.idiomatic: Terror for salary twists every word and deed."

sentences.chinese: 〉臣願賜尚方斬馬劔，斷佞臣一人以厲其餘。」
sentences.literal: 〉Your servant begs to be granted the Palace Workshop's horse-beheading sword to strike off one fawning minister and warn the rest."
sentences.idiomatic: Give me the imperial blade reserved for traitors—one stroke on a sycophant to sober the rest."

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「尚方，少府之屬官也，作供御器物，故有斬馬劔，劔利可以斬馬也。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Shangfang is a sub-bureau of the Junior Minister; it makes objects for imperial use, hence the horse-beheading sword—so sharp it can sever a horse."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: Shangfang forges the court's arms, including the ceremonial sword sharp enough to cleave a charger.

sentences.literal: 〉The emperor asked, "Who?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor snapped, "Name him."

sentences.literal: He answered, "Marquis of Anchang Zhang Yu."
sentences.idiomatic: "Zhang Yu of Anchang."

sentences.chinese: 上大怒，曰：「小臣居下訕上，〈師古曰：「訕，謗也，音所諫反，又音刪。」
sentences.literal: The emperor was greatly angered and said, "A petty clerk below slanders those above—〈Yan's note: "Shan means to revile; read suo-jian fan, also read shan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Cheng boiled over: "A low clerk defames his betters—Yan Shigu glosses shan as revile."

sentences.literal: 〉You have humiliated the tutor in open court—death without pardon."
sentences.idiomatic: You have insulted my teacher in the hall—no pardon."

sentences.chinese: 御史將雲下，雲攀殿檻，檻折。
sentences.literal: The Imperial Clerk led Yun down; Yun seized the palace threshold; the threshold broke.
sentences.idiomatic: Guards dragged Zhu away; he locked arms on the door sill until the carved rail snapped.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Kan is the railing before the hall."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: kan is the hall railing.

sentences.chinese: 〉雲呼曰：〈師古曰：「呼，叫也，音火故反。」
sentences.literal: 〉Yun cried out—〈Yan's note: "Hu means to shout; read huo-gu fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu roared—Yan Shigu glosses hu as shout.

sentences.chinese: 〉「臣得下從龍逢、比干遊於地下，足矣！
sentences.literal: 〉"If your servant may follow Long Peng and Prince Bi Gan in the world below, I am content!
sentences.idiomatic: "Let me join Long Peng and Bi Gan in the grave—I am content!

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「關龍逢，桀臣，王子比干，紂之諸父，皆以諫而死，故云然。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Guan Longfeng was Jie's minister; Prince Bi Gan was Zhou's uncle—both died remonstrating, hence he speaks so."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu names the two martyred remonstrators of Jie and Zhou.

sentences.literal: 〉I do not yet know what this sage court will be like!"
sentences.idiomatic: What your sage dynasty will look like, I cannot yet say!"

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "It means killing straight ministers brings an ill name."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: executing honest critics stains the throne.

sentences.literal: 〉Thereupon the clerk led Yun away.
sentences.idiomatic: The guards marched him off.

sentences.chinese: 於是左將軍辛慶忌免冠解印綬，叩頭殿下曰：「此臣素著狂直於世。
sentences.literal: Then General of the Left Xin Qingji removed his cap, loosed his seals and ribbons, and knocked his head below the dais, saying, "This subject has long been known to the world for mad bluntness.
sentences.idiomatic: Left general Xin Qingji tore off cap and sash and kowtowed: "The man is famous for wild integrity."

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Zhu means to display."
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: zhu means "display" or "show forth."

sentences.literal: It means this name has long been displayed abroad."〉
sentences.idiomatic: His reputation has stood for years."

sentences.literal: 〉If his words are right, he must not be executed;
sentences.idiomatic: If he speaks truth, spare him;

sentences.literal: if his words are wrong, he should still be indulged.
sentences.idiomatic: if he is wrong, still indulge his bluntness.

sentences.literal: Your servant dares contend for him even at the cost of death."
sentences.idiomatic: I will plead for him on pain of death."

sentences.literal: Qingji knocked his head until it bled.
sentences.idiomatic: Xin battered his brow on the stone until blood ran.

sentences.literal: The emperor's anger eased, and only then was the matter stopped.
sentences.idiomatic: Cheng's rage cooled and the execution halted.

sentences.literal: Later, when they were about to repair the threshold, the emperor said, "Do not replace it!
sentences.idiomatic: When carpenters came to mend the rail, the emperor said, "Leave it broken!

sentences.literal: Rather join it up as it is, to mark a straight-speaking minister."
sentences.idiomatic: Patch the break and keep it as a monument to blunt loyalty."

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「輯與集同，謂補合之也。
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Ji is the same as ji 'to gather'—meaning to mend and join."
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu reads ji like ji, to patch together.

sentences.literal: Jing means to display."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Jing means to set forth for all to see."

sentences.chinese: 雲自是之後不復仕，常居鄠田，時出乘牛車從諸生，所過皆敬事焉。
sentences.literal: After this Yun never again took office; he constantly dwelt on his lands at Hu, sometimes riding out in an ox-cart among the scholars, and wherever he passed they treated him with respect.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun never served again. He farmed at Hu, sometimes toured in an ox-cart with students, and every village honored him.

sentences.literal: When Xue Xuan became chancellor, Yun went to see him.
sentences.idiomatic: When Xue Xuan took the chancellorship, Zhu called on him.

sentences.chinese: 宣備賔主禮，因留雲宿，從容謂雲曰：〈師古曰：「從音七庸反。」
sentences.literal: Xuan prepared full host-and-guest courtesy, then detained Yun overnight and, at leisure, said to Yun—〈Yan's note: "Cong is read qi-yong fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Xue laid a feast, kept him overnight, and over wine said—Yan Shigu gives fanqie for cong.

sentences.chinese: 〉「在田野亡事，且留我東閤，可以觀四方竒士。」
sentences.literal: 〉"You have nothing to do in the fields—stay awhile in my east pavilion and you may watch the unusual talents of the four quarters."
sentences.idiomatic: "Idle in the country—lodge in my east wing and watch the empire's odd talents pass through."

sentences.literal: Yun said, "Does a young sprout mean to make me his clerk?"
sentences.idiomatic: "You fresh-faced stripling—you think you can press me into clerking for you?"

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Xiao sheng means a newly advanced student.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: xiao sheng is a junior scholar.

sentences.literal: Does it mean you wish to use me as a clerk?"〉
sentences.idiomatic: Are you trying to hire me as a secretary?"

sentences.literal: 〉Xuan dared speak no more.
sentences.idiomatic: Xue never raised it again.

sentences.chinese: 其敎授，擇諸生，然後爲弟子。
sentences.literal: In his teaching he chose among the scholars and only then made disciples.
sentences.idiomatic: He picked disciples only from the worthiest students.

sentences.chinese: 九江嚴望及望兄子元，字仲，能傳雲學，皆爲博士。
sentences.literal: Yan Wang of Jiujiang and Wang's nephew Yuan, courtesy Zhong, could transmit Yun's learning; both became erudites.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Wang of Jiujiang and his nephew Yan Yuan mastered Zhu's school; both rose to erudite.

sentences.literal: Wang rose to Guardian of Taishan.
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Wang governed Taishan.

sentences.literal: Yun lived past seventy and ended his days at home.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Yun died at home past seventy.

sentences.literal: When ill he did not summon physicians or take medicine.
sentences.idiomatic: Sick, he refused doctors and drugs.

sentences.chinese: 遺言以身服斂，棺周於身，土周於椁，〈師古曰：「棺周於身，小棺裁容身也。
sentences.literal: He left instructions to be clothed for burial only to the body's measure, a coffin just fitting the frame, earth just enclosing the outer shell—〈Yan's note: "The coffin circles the body—a small coffin barely holding the frame.
sentences.idiomatic: His will demanded a snug shroud, a coffin no wider than his shoulders, a pit barely wider than the shell—Yan Shigu explains the frugal measure.

sentences.chinese: 土周於椁，冢壙裁容椁也。」
sentences.literal: Earth circles the outer shell—the grave pit barely holds the shell."〉
sentences.idiomatic: The shaft would only admit the inner box."

sentences.literal: 〉He asked for a mound one zhang five chi high, buried outside the eastern wall of Pingling.
sentences.idiomatic: He asked for a burial mound one zhang and five chi high east of Pingling's outer wall.

sentences.chinese: 梅福字子真，九江壽春人也。
sentences.literal: Mei Fu, courtesy name Zizhen, was a man of Shouchun in Jiujiang.
sentences.idiomatic: Mei Fu, courtesy Zizhen, came from Shouchun in Jiujiang.

sentences.chinese: 少學長安，明尚書、穀梁春秋，爲郡文學，補南昌尉。
sentences.literal: In youth he studied in Chang'an, mastering the Documents and the Guliang commentary on the Spring and Autumn Annals; he became literary scholar of the commandery and was appointed constable of Nanchang.
sentences.idiomatic: He studied in Chang'an, mastered the Shang shu and Guliang Annals, served as commandery instructor, then constable of Nanchang.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "A county in Yuzhang."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: a Yuzhang county.

sentences.chinese: 〉後去官歸壽春，數因縣道上言變事，〈師古曰：「附縣道之使而封奏也。
sentences.literal: 〉Later he left office and returned to Shouchun, repeatedly using county couriers to memorialize on portents—〈Yan's note: "He attached sealed memorials to county couriers."
sentences.idiomatic: Home in Shouchun he kept tipping off the throne through county post riders—Yan Shigu: he hitched memorials to official couriers.

sentences.literal: Bian means extraordinary affairs."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Bian means crises or anomalies."

sentences.chinese: 〉求假軺傳，〈師古曰：「小車之傳也。
sentences.literal: 〉He begged leave to borrow a light carriage's relay tallies—〈Yan's note: "The relay tallies of a light cart."
sentences.idiomatic: He asked for relay tallies on a light wagon—Yan Shigu explains the small-carriage chop.

sentences.literal: Yao is read yao.
sentences.idiomatic: Yao is read yao.

sentences.literal: Zhuan is read zhang-lian fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Zhuan is read zhang-lian in fanqie."

sentences.chinese: 〉詣行在所條對急政，〈師古曰：「條對者，一一條錄而對之。」
sentences.literal: 〉to go to the imperial encampment and itemize answers on urgent policy—〈Yan's note: "Tiao dui means to list each item and answer."〉
sentences.idiomatic: to reach the temporary palace and answer point by point on urgent reforms—Yan Shigu glosses tiao dui as itemized response.

sentences.literal: 〉Each time he was answered with dismissal.
sentences.idiomatic: Each time the court brushed him off.

sentences.chinese: 是時成帝委任大將軍王鳳，鳳專埶擅朝，而京兆尹王章素忠直，譏刺鳳，爲鳳所誅。
sentences.literal: At that time Emperor Cheng entrusted affairs to Grand General Wang Feng; Feng monopolized power and ran the court, while the Governor of the Capital Wang Zhang, always loyal and blunt, satirized Feng and was executed by Feng.
sentences.idiomatic: Cheng handed power to Wang Feng; Feng choked the court. Wang Zhang of the capital spoke sharp truth and Feng had him killed.

sentences.chinese: 王氏浸盛，〈師古曰：「浸，漸也。」
sentences.literal: The Wang clan grew ever stronger—〈Yan's note: "Jin means gradually."〉
sentences.idiomatic: The Wangs swelled—Yan Shigu: jin is "step by step."

sentences.literal: 〉portents multiplied, yet none below dared speak straight.
sentences.idiomatic: Omens stacked up while every tongue stayed tied.

sentences.literal: Fu again submitted a memorial saying:
sentences.idiomatic: Mei Fu memorialized again:

sentences.chinese: 臣聞箕子佯狂於殷，而爲周陳洪範；
sentences.literal: Your servant has heard that Jizi feigned madness in Yin yet laid out the Great Plan for Zhou;
sentences.idiomatic: Jizi played the madman under Shang yet gave Zhou the Hong fan;

sentences.chinese: 叔孫通遁秦歸漢，制作儀品。
sentences.literal: Shusun Tong fled Qin and returned to Han, framing ritual and regulations.
sentences.idiomatic: Shusun Tong slipped from Qin to Han and built the court's ritual code.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Dun means to flee."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: dun is flee.

sentences.chinese: 〉夫叔孫先非不忠也，〈師古曰：「先猶言先生也。
sentences.literal: 〉Now Shusun Tong was not disloyal—〈Yan's note: "Xian means sir or elder."
sentences.idiomatic: Shusun Tong was no traitor—Yan Shigu glosses xian as "sir" or "venerable."

sentences.literal: One opinion says xian refers to his time in Qin."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Another gloss: xian means "while still in Qin."

sentences.chinese: 〉箕子非疏其家而畔親也，〈師古曰：「箕子，紂之諸父，故言疏家畔親也。」
sentences.literal: 〉Jizi did not cast off his kin to rebel against kin—〈Yan's note: "Jizi was Zhou's uncle, hence 'cast off kin to rebel against kin.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: Jizi did not betray family to turn traitor—Yan Shigu reminds us he was the king's uncle.

sentences.literal: 〉it was impossible to get a hearing.
sentences.idiomatic: No one would listen.

sentences.chinese: 昔高祖納善若不及，從諫若轉圜，〈師古曰：「不及，恐失之也。
sentences.literal: Formerly Gaozu welcomed good counsel as if he could never catch up, followed remonstrance as easily as turning a round object—〈Yan's note: "Bu ji means fearing to miss it.
sentences.idiomatic: Gaozu drank in advice for fear of missing a word and spun on a remonstrance like a ball—Yan Shigu: bu ji is dread of losing counsel.

sentences.literal: Zhuan yuan means compliant."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Zhuan yuan means turning a round weight—easy motion, ready compliance."

sentences.chinese: 〉聽言不求其能，舉功不考其素。
sentences.literal: 〉He listened to words without demanding talent, rewarded deeds without investigating origins.
sentences.idiomatic: He heard men out without demanding polish and rewarded deeds without probing pedigrees.

sentences.chinese: 〈師古曰：「直取其功，不論其舊行及所從來也。」
sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "He took the merit alone, not debating old conduct or where they came from."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: take the deed at face value, not the résumé.

sentences.chinese: 〉陳平起於亡命而爲謀主，韓信拔於行陳而建上將。
sentences.literal: 〉Chen Ping rose from fugitive to become chief planner; Han Xin was pulled from the ranks and made supreme general.
sentences.idiomatic: Chen Ping rose from exile as strategist; Han Xin leaped from the ranks to grand marshal.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "He was appointed grand general."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: named grand general of the armies.

sentences.chinese: 〉故天下之士雲合歸漢，〈師古曰：「言四面而至。」
sentences.literal: 〉Therefore knights of the empire flocked to Han like clouds—〈Yan's note: "It means they came from all sides."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Talent poured to Han from every quarter—Yan Shigu: like clouds converging.

sentences.chinese: 〉爭進竒異，知者竭其策，愚者盡其慮，勇士極其節，怯夫勉其死。
sentences.literal: 〉They vied to offer strange plans: the wise exhausted their schemes, the foolish poured out their anxieties, the brave spent their utmost integrity, the coward steeled himself to face death.
sentences.idiomatic: Wise men laid schemes, fools found courage, heroes spent their nerve, cowards learned to die.

sentences.chinese: 合天下之知，并天下之威，是以舉秦如鴻毛，取楚若拾遺，〈師古曰：「鴻毛喻輕。
sentences.literal: They pooled the wit of the realm and the majesty of the realm—therefore they lifted Qin as lightly as a goose feather and took Chu like picking up lost goods—〈Yan's note: "Goose feather means light.
sentences.idiomatic: All minds and all awe united—Qin fell like fluff, Chu like snatching dropped coins—Yan Shigu: goose feather for lightness.

sentences.literal: Shi yi means easy."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Shi yi means as easy as stooping for a trifle."

sentences.literal: 〉This is how Gaozu had no match under Heaven.
sentences.idiomatic: That is how Gaozu became unbeatable.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Wu is read the same as wu 'without.'"〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu reads wu like wu, "without peer."

sentences.chinese: 〉孝文皇帝起於代谷，〈師古曰：「從代而來即帝位。」
sentences.literal: 〉Emperor Wen rose from the Dai valley—〈Yan's note: "He came from Dai to take the throne."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Emperor Wen came from the Dai commandery to the throne—Yan Shigu notes his path.

sentences.chinese: 〉非有周召之師，伊呂之佐也，〈師古曰：「召讀曰邵。」
sentences.literal: 〉without the tutors of Zhou and Shao or the assistants of Yi and Lu—〈Yan's note: "Shao is read shao."〉
sentences.idiomatic: without the Zhou-Shao tutors or Yi-Lu ministers—Yan Shigu: Shao reads shao.

sentences.literal: 〉he followed Gaozu's laws and added reverence and frugality.
sentences.idiomatic: He kept Gaozu's code and layered on modest thrift.

sentences.literal: At that time the realm was almost at peace.
sentences.idiomatic: The realm neared peace.

sentences.literal: 〈Yan's note: "Ji is read ju-yi fan."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Yan Shigu: ji is read ju-yi.

sentences.chinese: 〉繇是言之，循高祖之法則治，不循則亂。
sentences.literal: 〉From this speaking, following Gaozu's laws brings order; not following brings chaos.
sentences.idiomatic: Follow Gaozu's pattern and the land rests; abandon it and the land boils.

sentences.literal: Why?
sentences.idiomatic: Why?

sentences.chinese: 秦爲亡道，削仲尼之迹，滅周公之軌，〈師古曰：「軌，法也。」
sentences.literal: Qin practiced lawless ways, pared away Confucius's tracks, destroyed the Duke of Zhou's norms—〈Yan's note: "Gui means model."〉
sentences.idiomatic: Qin hacked away Confucius, smashed the Duke of Zhou's models—Yan Shigu: gui is "pattern."

sentences.chinese: 〉壞井田，除五等，禮廢樂崩，王道不通，故欲行王道者莫能致其功也。
sentences.literal: 〉destroyed well-field grids, abolished the five ranks, ritual collapsed and music shattered, the kingly way was blocked—therefore whoever wished to enact the kingly way could not achieve its merit.
sentences.idiomatic: It tore up well-fields and the five ranks until rites and music died and the royal way clogged—so no later king could finish the work.
